rawHead:
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Large deviations</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false,
      macros:{
        '\\Spec':'\\Spec',
        '\\Gal':'\\Gal',
        '\\Hom':'\\Hom',
        '\\tr':'\\tr',
        '\\ad':'\\ad',
        '\\ind':'\\ind'
      }
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>
<!-- breadcrumb-head-auto-begin -->

rawBodyPrefix:
← Notebook
1 Cramér 2 Rate functions 3 Sanov 4 Gärtner–Ellis 5 Applications

Large deviations

The law of large numbers says $\bar X_n\to\mu$. Large-deviation theory asks how unlikely the rare excursion $\bar X_n\approx x\ne\mu$ actually is — and answers $\mathbb{P}(\bar X_n\approx x)\asymp e^{-nI(x)}$, where the rate function $I$ is the Legendre transform of the log-MGF. Cramér, Sanov, Gärtner–Ellis, Schilder, and Freidlin–Wentzell are five faces of one principle.

blocks.html:
1. Cramér's theorem

Let $X_1,X_2,\ldots$ be i.i.d.\ real random variables with finite log-MGF $$\Lambda(\theta)=\log\mathbb{E}[e^{\theta X_1}]<\infty\quad\text{for }\theta\text{ in a neighbourhood of }0,$$ and write $\bar X_n=\tfrac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n X_i$ and $\mu=\mathbb{E}[X_1]$. The strong law gives $\bar X_n\to\mu$ a.s.; Cramér's theorem quantifies the rare event $\{\bar X_n\ge x\}$ for $x>\mu$: $$\lim_{n\to\infty}\frac{1}{n}\log\mathbb{P}(\bar X_n\ge x)=-I(x),\qquad I(x)=\sup_{\theta\in\mathbb{R}}\bigl\{\theta x-\Lambda(\theta)\bigr\}.$$ The right-hand side is the Legendre–Fenchel transform of $\Lambda$. Tail probabilities decay exponentially in $n$ at rate $I(x)$.

Two-sided: the same identity holds for $\mathbb{P}(\bar X_n\le x)$ when $x<\mu$, and the joint statement $\mathbb{P}(\bar X_n\in F)\lesssim e^{-n\inf_F I}$ (closed $F$), $\mathbb{P}(\bar X_n\in G)\gtrsim e^{-n\inf_G I}$ (open $G$) is the large-deviation principle (LDP) — the canonical packaging.

Why a Legendre transform? The exponential tilt $\mathbb{P}_\theta(dx)=e^{\theta x-\Lambda(\theta)}\mathbb{P}(dx)$ makes $x$ the typical mean: choose $\theta=\theta^*$ so $\Lambda'(\theta^*)=x$. Under $\mathbb{P}_\theta$ the event becomes typical and a Radon–Nikodym change of measure costs $e^{-n(\theta x-\Lambda(\theta))}=e^{-nI(x)}$. The Legendre sup is "pick the cheapest tilt that makes $x$ typical."

Worked Gaussian example. If $X_i\sim\mathcal{N}(0,1)$, then $\Lambda(\theta)=\theta^2/2$. Maximising $\theta x-\theta^2/2$ gives $\theta^*=x$ and $I(x)=x^2/2$. So $\mathbb{P}(\bar X_n\ge x)\sim e^{-nx^2/2}$ — exactly the Gaussian tail bound, but now the constant is sharp on the log scale.

Bernoulli example. If $X_i\sim\text{Bernoulli}(p)$, $\Lambda(\theta)=\log(1-p+pe^\theta)$ and $$I(x)=x\log\tfrac{x}{p}+(1-x)\log\tfrac{1-x}{1-p}=H(\text{Ber}(x)\,\|\,\text{Ber}(p))$$ on $[0,1]$ — exactly the Kullback–Leibler divergence. Coin tossing is the simplest non-Gaussian instance and already exposes the link to information theory.

Cramér rate via Legendre tilt
drag the slider — see the supporting line $\theta x-\Lambda(\theta)$

See also
  • Probability theory · Expectation, variance, and moments
  • Probability theory · Law of large numbers

Used in
Large deviations · Rate functions and convex duality

<script>
/* =============================== §1 Cramér: Legendre transform =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = $("#cramer-svg"), out = $("#cr-out");
  const dist = $("#cr-dist"), xs = $("#cr-x"), xv = $("#cr-x-v");
  const W = 760, H = 320, padL = 60, padR = 30, padT = 30, padB = 50;
  // inner plot is the Legendre construction in (theta, value)-space.
  const tMin = -2, tMax = 2;
  function lambda(theta, kind){
    if(kind === "gauss") return theta*theta/2;
    if(kind === "bern")  return Math.log(0.5 + 0.5*Math.exp(theta));
    if(kind === "exp")   return theta < 1 ? -Math.log(1 - theta) : Infinity;
    return 0;
  }
  function lambdaDeriv(theta, kind){
    if(kind === "gauss") return theta;
    if(kind === "bern"){ const e = Math.exp(theta); return e/(1+e); }
    if(kind === "exp")   return theta < 1 ? 1/(1 - theta) : Infinity;
    return 0;
  }
  // numerical Legendre sup
  function rate(x, kind){
    let best = -Infinity, bestT = 0;
    const N = 800;
    for(let i = 0; i <= N; i++){
      const t = tMin + (tMax - tMin)*i/N;
      const v = t*x - lambda(t, kind);
      if(isFinite(v) && v > best){ best = v; bestT = t; }
    }
    return {I: best, theta: bestT};
  }
  function px(t){ return padL + (t - tMin)/(tMax - tMin) * (W - padL - padR); }
  function py(v, vMin, vMax){ return H - padB - (v - vMin)/(vMax - vMin) * (H - padT - padB); }
  function draw(){
    const kind = dist.value;
    const x = +xs.value;
    xv.textContent = x.toFixed(2);
    svg.innerHTML = "";
    // determine y-range from samples of Lambda and theta*x
    let vMin = Infinity, vMax = -Infinity;
    const samples = [];
    const N = 240;
    for(let i = 0; i <= N; i++){
      const t = tMin + (tMax - tMin)*i/N;
      const Lt = lambda(t, kind);
      const Tx = t*x;
      samples.push({t, Lt, Tx});
      if(isFinite(Lt)){ vMin = Math.min(vMin, Lt); vMax = Math.max(vMax, Lt); }
      if(isFinite(Tx)){ vMin = Math.min(vMin, Tx); vMax = Math.max(vMax, Tx); }
    }
    vMin = Math.min(vMin, -2); vMax = Math.max(vMax, 3);
    // axes
    const xAxisY = py(0, vMin, vMax);
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL,y1:xAxisY,x2:W-padR,y2:xAxisY,stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:px(0),y1:padT,x2:px(0),y2:H-padB,stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    // axis labels
    let lbl = SVG("text",{x:W-padR,y:xAxisY-6,"text-anchor":"end","font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    lbl.textContent = "θ"; svg.appendChild(lbl);
    lbl = SVG("text",{x:px(0)+6,y:padT+12,"text-anchor":"start","font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    lbl.textContent = "value"; svg.appendChild(lbl);
    // Lambda(theta) curve
    let d = "";
    for(const s of samples){
      if(!isFinite(s.Lt)) continue;
      d += (d ? "L" : "M") + px(s.t) + " " + py(s.Lt, vMin, vMax) + " ";
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--cyan)", "stroke-width":2}));
    // theta*x line (the affine being maximised)
    let d2 = "";
    for(const s of samples){
      d2 += (d2 ? "L" : "M") + px(s.t) + " " + py(s.Tx, vMin, vMax) + " ";
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:d2, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--violet)", "stroke-width":1.5, "stroke-dasharray":"4 4"}));
    // sup gap = theta*x - Lambda(theta)
    let d3 = "";
    for(const s of samples){
      if(!isFinite(s.Lt)) continue;
      d3 += (d3 ? "L" : "M") + px(s.t) + " " + py(s.Tx - s.Lt, vMin, vMax) + " ";
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:d3, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--yellow)", "stroke-width":2}));
    // mark the maximiser
    const r = rate(x, kind);
    const mx = px(r.theta), myG = py(r.theta*x - lambda(r.theta, kind), vMin, vMax);
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:mx, cy:myG, r:5, fill:"var(--yellow)"}));
    // vertical drop showing I(x)
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:mx, y1:myG, x2:mx, y2:py(0, vMin, vMax),
      stroke:"var(--yellow)", "stroke-dasharray":"3 3"}));
    // legend
    const legend = [
      {color:"var(--cyan)",   label:"Λ(θ) log-MGF"},
      {color:"var(--violet)", label:"θ·x affine"},
      {color:"var(--yellow)", label:"θx − Λ(θ)"}
    ];
    legend.forEach((it, i) => {
      const ly = padT + 8 + i*16;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:W-padR-150, y:ly-9, width:14, height:3, fill:it.color}));
      const t = SVG("text",{x:W-padR-130, y:ly-3, "font-size":11, fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = it.label;
      svg.appendChild(t);
    });
    out.textContent =
      "Λ(θ*) = " + lambda(r.theta, kind).toFixed(4) + ", θ* = " + r.theta.toFixed(3) + "\n" +
      "I(x) = sup_θ [θ·x − Λ(θ)] = " + r.I.toFixed(4) + "\n" +
      "P(̄X_n ≥ x) ≍ exp(−n · " + r.I.toFixed(3) + ")";
  }
  dist.addEventListener("change", draw);
  xs.addEventListener("input", draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>
</section>

2. Rate functions and convex duality

Cramér gives a recipe; the rate function $I$ has structural properties worth isolating. A (good) rate function on a Polish space $\mathcal{X}$ is $I:\mathcal{X}\to[0,\infty]$ with:

  • Lower semicontinuity: level sets $\{x:I(x)\le\alpha\}$ are closed.
  • Compactness of level sets (the good qualifier): closed and bounded in $\mathcal{X}$.
  • Vanishing at the LLN limit: $I(\mu)=0$, since $\theta=0$ achieves the sup when $\Lambda'(0)=\mu$.

When $I=\Lambda^*$ for a convex $\Lambda$, the Legendre involution $(\Lambda^*)^*=\Lambda$ holds wherever $\Lambda$ is convex and lower semicontinuous. Geometrically, $\Lambda$ and $I$ are dual: each is the supporting-hyperplane envelope of the other.

Quadratic example. Gaussian: $\Lambda(\theta)=\sigma^2\theta^2/2+\mu\theta\Rightarrow I(x)=(x-\mu)^2/(2\sigma^2)$. The rate function is the squared Mahalanobis distance — Gaussian tails are exactly quadratic.

Bernoulli/binomial. $I(x)=H(\text{Ber}(x)\,\|\,\text{Ber}(p))$ on $[0,1]$, $+\infty$ off $[0,1]$. The infinite values force support: the empirical mean cannot exceed $1$, no matter how rare we are willing to consider.

Exponential. If $X_i\sim\text{Exp}(1)$ (mean $1$), $\Lambda(\theta)=-\log(1-\theta)$ for $\theta<1$ and $I(x)=x-1-\log x$ on $(0,\infty)$ — the Itakura–Saito divergence from $1$. Note $I(x)$ is asymmetric: large $x$ is more punishing than small $x$.

Reading off probabilities. If $I$ is strictly convex with minimum at $\mu$, a quadratic Taylor expansion $I(x)\approx\tfrac{1}{2}I''(\mu)(x-\mu)^2$ recovers the central limit theorem: $\sqrt n(\bar X_n-\mu)\Rightarrow\mathcal{N}(0,1/I''(\mu))$. The CLT is the LDP linearised at $\mu$, and $I''(\mu)=1/\sigma^2$.

Rate-function gallery
compare $I$ across families and zoom on the LLN minimum

Used in
Large deviations · Gärtner–Ellis theorem
Large deviations · Sanov's theorem

<script>
/* =============================== §2 Rate-function gallery =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = $("#rate-svg"), out = $("#r-out");
  const zoom = $("#r-zoom"), zv = $("#r-zoom-v");
  const W = 760, H = 320, padL = 50, padR = 30, padT = 25, padB = 45;
  const families = {
    gauss:   {mean:0, fn:x=> x*x/2,                   xMin:-3, xMax:3,   label:"Gaussian"},
    bern:    {mean:0.5, fn:x=> (x<=0||x>=1)? Infinity : x*Math.log(2*x)+(1-x)*Math.log(2*(1-x)),
              xMin:0, xMax:1,   label:"Bernoulli p=½"},
    exp:     {mean:1, fn:x=> x<=0 ? Infinity : x - 1 - Math.log(x), xMin:0.05, xMax:4, label:"Exp(1)"},
    poisson: {mean:1, fn:x=> x<=0 ? Infinity : x*Math.log(x) - x + 1, xMin:0.05, xMax:4, label:"Poisson(1)"}
  };
  let active = "gauss";
  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML = "";
    const F = families[active];
    const z = +zoom.value;
    zv.textContent = "×" + (1 + 4*z).toFixed(2);
    // x range collapses toward the mean as z→1
    const half = (F.xMax - F.xMin)/2 / (1 + 4*z);
    const xMin = F.mean - half, xMax = F.mean + half;
    const N = 320;
    let yMax = 0;
    const pts = [];
    for(let i = 0; i <= N; i++){
      const x = xMin + (xMax - xMin)*i/N;
      const y = F.fn(x);
      pts.push({x, y});
      if(isFinite(y)) yMax = Math.max(yMax, y);
    }
    yMax = Math.max(yMax, 0.4);
    function px(x){ return padL + (x - xMin)/(xMax - xMin) * (W - padL - padR); }
    function py(y){ return H - padB - Math.min(y, yMax)/yMax * (H - padT - padB); }
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL,y1:H-padB,x2:W-padR,y2:H-padB,stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL,y1:padT,x2:padL,y2:H-padB,stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    // gridlines / ticks
    for(let k = 0; k <= 4; k++){
      const yv = yMax*k/4;
      const yp = py(yv);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL, y1:yp, x2:W-padR, y2:yp,
        stroke:"var(--line)", "stroke-dasharray":"2 4", opacity:0.4}));
      const t = SVG("text",{x:padL-8, y:yp+4, "text-anchor":"end", "font-size":10, fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = yv.toFixed(2);
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // x ticks
    for(let k = 0; k <= 4; k++){
      const xv = xMin + (xMax - xMin)*k/4;
      const xp = px(xv);
      const t = SVG("text",{x:xp, y:H-padB+14, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":10, fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = xv.toFixed(2);
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // curve
    let d = "";
    for(const p of pts){
      if(!isFinite(p.y)) continue;
      d += (d ? "L" : "M") + px(p.x) + " " + py(p.y) + " ";
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--yellow)", "stroke-width":2.2}));
    // mean marker
    const mp = px(F.mean);
    if(mp >= padL && mp <= W - padR){
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:mp, y1:padT, x2:mp, y2:H-padB,
        stroke:"var(--green)", "stroke-dasharray":"3 3", opacity:0.7}));
      const t = SVG("text",{x:mp + 4, y:padT + 12, "font-size":11, fill:"var(--green)"});
      t.textContent = "μ = " + F.mean;
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // axis labels
    const xl = SVG("text",{x:W - padR, y:H - padB - 6, "text-anchor":"end", "font-size":12, fill:"var(--mute)"});
    xl.textContent = "x"; svg.appendChild(xl);
    const yl = SVG("text",{x:padL + 6, y:padT + 12, "font-size":12, fill:"var(--mute)"});
    yl.textContent = "I(x)"; svg.appendChild(yl);
    // family label
    const fl = SVG("text",{x:W - padR - 10, y:padT + 14, "text-anchor":"end", "font-size":12, fill:"var(--yellow)", "font-weight":600});
    fl.textContent = F.label;
    svg.appendChild(fl);
    // sample I-values
    const xs = [F.mean - half/2, F.mean + half/2];
    out.textContent = "I(" + xs[0].toFixed(3) + ") = " + F.fn(xs[0]).toFixed(4) +
                      "    I(" + xs[1].toFixed(3) + ") = " + F.fn(xs[1]).toFixed(4) +
                      "\nI(μ) = " + F.fn(F.mean).toFixed(4) + "  (LLN minimum is 0)";
  }
  document.querySelectorAll("#w-rate .row button").forEach(b=>{
    b.addEventListener("click",()=>{
      document.querySelectorAll("#w-rate .row button").forEach(x=>x.classList.remove("active"));
      b.classList.add("active");
      active = b.id.replace("r-","");
      draw();
    });
  });
  zoom.addEventListener("input", draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>
</section>

3. Sanov's theorem and KL divergence

Cramér tracks the empirical mean $\bar X_n$. Sanov's theorem tracks the full empirical distribution $$L_n=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n\delta_{X_i}\in\mathcal{P}(\mathcal{X})$$ of i.i.d.\ samples from $\mu$. It is the LDP on the space of probability measures: for measurable $A\subset\mathcal{P}(\mathcal{X})$, $$\mathbb{P}(L_n\in A)\asymp\exp\Bigl(-n\inf_{\nu\in A}H(\nu\,\|\,\mu)\Bigr),$$ where $H(\nu\,\|\,\mu)=\int\log(d\nu/d\mu)\,d\nu$ (= $+\infty$ if $\nu\not\ll\mu$) is the Kullback–Leibler divergence.

This is the most basic instance of the slogan "entropy is the rate function of i.i.d.\ statistics." On a finite alphabet $\mathcal{X}=\{a_1,\ldots,a_k\}$, $L_n$ is just the histogram, and $$H(\nu\,\|\,\mu)=\sum_{i=1}^k\nu_i\log\frac{\nu_i}{\mu_i}.$$ By Pinsker, $H\ge 2\|\nu-\mu\|_{TV}^2$, so KL controls every weaker metric.

Cramér from Sanov via contraction. The empirical mean is a continuous functional of $L_n$: $\bar X_n=\int x\,dL_n(x)$. The contraction principle says: if $L_n$ satisfies an LDP with rate $H(\,\cdot\,\|\,\mu)$ and $f$ is continuous, then $f(L_n)$ has rate $I(y)=\inf\{H(\nu\,\|\,\mu):\int x\,d\nu(x)=y\}$. Solving the constrained minimisation by Lagrange multipliers reproduces $I(y)=\sup_\theta\{\theta y-\Lambda(\theta)\}$.

Worked example: biased coin. $\mu=\text{Ber}(1/2)$, target $\nu=\text{Ber}(p)$. Then $H(\nu\,\|\,\mu)=p\log(2p)+(1-p)\log(2(1-p))$. The probability that $n$ fair coin flips produce a fraction-of-heads close to $p=0.9$ is approximately $e^{-n\cdot 0.368}$. For $n=100$ this is $\approx e^{-36.8}\approx 10^{-16}$ — astronomically rare, but the rate is sharp.

Empirical histogram vs.\ KL rate
edit target $\nu$ on a 4-letter alphabet

Used in
Information theory · Kullback–Leibler divergence
Large deviations · Applications: Schilder, Freidlin–Wentzell, entropy

<script>
/* =============================== §3 Sanov: empirical histogram + KL =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = $("#sanov-svg"), out = $("#s-out");
  const q1 = $("#s-q1"), q2 = $("#s-q2"), q3 = $("#s-q3");
  const v1 = $("#s-q1-v"), v2 = $("#s-q2-v"), v3 = $("#s-q3-v");
  const W = 760, H = 280, padT = 30, padB = 60;
  const mu = [0.25, 0.25, 0.25, 0.25]; // reference uniform
  const labels = ["a","b","c","d"];
  function normalise(){
    let a = +q1.value, b = +q2.value, c = +q3.value;
    let d = 1 - a - b - c;
    if(d < 0.02){
      const s = a + b + c;
      const scale = 0.98/s;
      a *= scale; b *= scale; c *= scale; d = 0.02;
    }
    return [a, b, c, d];
  }
  function kl(nu, mu){
    let s = 0;
    for(let i = 0; i < nu.length; i++){
      if(nu[i] <= 0) continue;
      s += nu[i] * Math.log(nu[i]/mu[i]);
    }
    return s;
  }
  function draw(){
    const nu = normalise();
    v1.textContent = nu[0].toFixed(2);
    v2.textContent = nu[1].toFixed(2);
    v3.textContent = nu[2].toFixed(2);
    svg.innerHTML = "";
    // 2 grouped bars per letter: mu and nu
    const groupW = 130, gap = 40;
    const startX = (W - 4*groupW - 3*gap)/2;
    const barH = 160;
    const baseY = H - padB;
    const topY = baseY - barH;
    // baseline
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:30, y1:baseY, x2:W-30, y2:baseY, stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    for(let i = 0; i < 4; i++){
      const gx = startX + i*(groupW + gap);
      const muH = mu[i] * barH;
      const nuH = nu[i] * barH;
      // mu bar (left half)
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:gx, y:baseY - muH, width:groupW/2 - 4, height:muH,
        fill:"var(--violet)", opacity:0.85}));
      // nu bar (right half)
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:gx + groupW/2 + 4, y:baseY - nuH, width:groupW/2 - 4, height:nuH,
        fill:"var(--yellow)", opacity:0.9}));
      // letter label
      const lt = SVG("text",{x:gx + groupW/2, y:baseY + 18, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":13, fill:"var(--ink)", "font-weight":600});
      lt.textContent = labels[i];
      svg.appendChild(lt);
      // value labels
      const mt = SVG("text",{x:gx + groupW/4 - 2, y:baseY - muH - 4, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":10, fill:"var(--violet)"});
      mt.textContent = mu[i].toFixed(2);
      svg.appendChild(mt);
      const nt = SVG("text",{x:gx + 3*groupW/4 + 4, y:baseY - nuH - 4, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":10, fill:"var(--yellow)"});
      nt.textContent = nu[i].toFixed(2);
      svg.appendChild(nt);
    }
    // legend at top
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:50, y:padT, width:14, height:10, fill:"var(--violet)"}));
    let lt = SVG("text",{x:70, y:padT + 9, "font-size":11, fill:"var(--mute)"});
    lt.textContent = "μ (uniform reference)";
    svg.appendChild(lt);
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:240, y:padT, width:14, height:10, fill:"var(--yellow)"}));
    lt = SVG("text",{x:260, y:padT + 9, "font-size":11, fill:"var(--mute)"});
    lt.textContent = "ν (target)";
    svg.appendChild(lt);
    const klv = kl(nu, mu);
    // P(L_n ≈ ν) ≈ e^{-n·KL}
    const ns = [10, 100, 1000];
    const probs = ns.map(n => "n=" + n + ": ≍ exp(−" + (n*klv).toFixed(2) + ") ≈ " +
      (n*klv > 30 ? "e^−" + (n*klv).toFixed(1) : Math.exp(-n*klv).toExponential(2))).join("    ");
    out.textContent = "H(ν‖μ) = " + klv.toFixed(4) + " nats    " + probs +
      "\nUniform μ achieves H = 0; the further ν is from μ in KL, the rarer the empirical histogram.";
  }
  q1.addEventListener("input", draw);
  q2.addEventListener("input", draw);
  q3.addEventListener("input", draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>
</section>

4. Gärtner–Ellis: beyond i.i.d.

Cramér assumes i.i.d.\ summands, which is a strong restriction. The Gärtner–Ellis theorem replaces "i.i.d.\ log-MGF" with a limiting log-MGF at scale $n$: $$\Lambda(\theta)\ :=\ \lim_{n\to\infty}\frac{1}{n}\log\mathbb{E}\bigl[e^{n\theta\bar X_n}\bigr].$$ If this limit exists, is finite on a neighbourhood of $0$, and is essentially smooth (differentiable in the interior of its effective domain with $|\Lambda'|\to\infty$ at the boundary), then $\bar X_n$ satisfies an LDP with rate $I=\Lambda^*$.

This covers a wide zoo: ergodic Markov chains (where $\Lambda$ comes from the Perron eigenvalue of the tilted transition operator), stationary Gaussian processes (where $\Lambda$ involves the spectral density), and bounded weakly dependent sums.

Worked example: AR(1) chain. Let $X_n=\rho X_{n-1}+\xi_n$ with $\xi_n\sim\mathcal{N}(0,1)$ i.i.d., stationary mean $0$, variance $1/(1-\rho^2)$. The limiting log-MGF for $\bar X_n$ is $$\Lambda(\theta)=\frac{\theta^2}{2(1-\rho)^2},\qquad I(x)=\frac{(1-\rho)^2 x^2}{2}.$$ Persistence ($\rho>0$) flattens the rate function — large deviations become less rare because the chain "drifts" coherently rather than averaging out.

Why essential smoothness? Without it, the upper bound of the LDP still holds, but the lower bound can fail at exposed points $x$ where $I(x)$ is achieved only at the boundary of $\Lambda$'s domain. A classic counterexample: $\Lambda$ piecewise linear (e.g.\ for rare-event-conditioned chains) — then $\Lambda^*$ has flat regions and some atypical values are unreachable by tilting.

AR(1): how persistence reshapes $I(x)$
$\rho=0$ recovers Cramér; $\rho\to 1$ flattens

See also
  • Probability theory · Modes of convergence

Used in
Large deviations · Applications: Schilder, Freidlin–Wentzell, entropy

<script>
/* =============================== §4 Gärtner–Ellis: AR(1) =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = $("#gartner-svg"), out = $("#g-out");
  const rho = $("#g-rho"), rv = $("#g-rho-v");
  const xs = $("#g-x"), xv = $("#g-x-v");
  const W = 760, H = 320, padL = 60, padR = 40, padT = 25, padB = 50;
  const xMin = -3, xMax = 3;
  function rateFor(r){
    const k = (1 - r)*(1 - r) / 2;
    return x => k*x*x;
  }
  function px(x){ return padL + (x - xMin)/(xMax - xMin) * (W - padL - padR); }
  function draw(){
    const r = +rho.value;
    const x = +xs.value;
    rv.textContent = r.toFixed(2);
    xv.textContent = x.toFixed(2);
    svg.innerHTML = "";
    const yMax = 4;
    function py(y){ return H - padB - Math.min(y, yMax)/yMax * (H - padT - padB); }
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL, y1:H-padB, x2:W-padR, y2:H-padB, stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL, y1:padT, x2:padL, y2:H-padB, stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    // gridlines
    for(let k = 1; k <= 4; k++){
      const yp = py(k);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL, y1:yp, x2:W-padR, y2:yp,
        stroke:"var(--line)", "stroke-dasharray":"2 4", opacity:0.4}));
      const t = SVG("text",{x:padL-8, y:yp+4, "text-anchor":"end", "font-size":10, fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = String(k);
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    for(let k = -3; k <= 3; k++){
      const xp = px(k);
      const t = SVG("text",{x:xp, y:H-padB+14, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":10, fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = String(k);
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // ghost rho=0 (Cramér / Gaussian)
    const ghostFn = rateFor(0);
    let dg = "";
    for(let i = 0; i <= 240; i++){
      const xx = xMin + (xMax - xMin)*i/240;
      dg += (dg ? "L" : "M") + px(xx) + " " + py(ghostFn(xx)) + " ";
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:dg, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--mute)", "stroke-width":1.4, "stroke-dasharray":"4 4"}));
    // active rate function
    const fn = rateFor(r);
    let d = "";
    for(let i = 0; i <= 240; i++){
      const xx = xMin + (xMax - xMin)*i/240;
      d += (d ? "L" : "M") + px(xx) + " " + py(fn(xx)) + " ";
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--cyan)", "stroke-width":2.4}));
    // marker at target x
    const mp = px(x), mq = py(fn(x));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:mp, cy:mq, r:5, fill:"var(--yellow)"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:mp, y1:mq, x2:mp, y2:py(0),
      stroke:"var(--yellow)", "stroke-dasharray":"3 3"}));
    // axis labels and legend
    let l = SVG("text",{x:W-padR, y:H-padB-6, "text-anchor":"end", "font-size":12, fill:"var(--mute)"});
    l.textContent = "x"; svg.appendChild(l);
    l = SVG("text",{x:padL+6, y:padT+12, "font-size":12, fill:"var(--mute)"});
    l.textContent = "I(x)"; svg.appendChild(l);
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:W-padR-180, y:padT, width:14, height:3, fill:"var(--mute)"}));
    l = SVG("text",{x:W-padR-160, y:padT+6, "font-size":11, fill:"var(--mute)"});
    l.textContent = "ρ=0 (i.i.d.)"; svg.appendChild(l);
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:W-padR-180, y:padT+18, width:14, height:3, fill:"var(--cyan)"}));
    l = SVG("text",{x:W-padR-160, y:padT+24, "font-size":11, fill:"var(--cyan)"});
    l.textContent = "ρ=" + r.toFixed(2); svg.appendChild(l);
    out.textContent =
      "Λ(θ) = θ²/(2(1−ρ)²),  I(x) = (1−ρ)²·x²/2\n" +
      "ρ = " + r.toFixed(3) + ",  I(" + x.toFixed(2) + ") = " + fn(x).toFixed(4) +
      "    (i.i.d. baseline I(x) = " + ghostFn(x).toFixed(4) + ")\n" +
      "Persistence ratio I_ρ / I_0 = " + (fn(x)/Math.max(ghostFn(x),1e-9)).toFixed(3);
  }
  rho.addEventListener("input", draw);
  xs.addEventListener("input", draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>
</section>

5. Applications: Schilder, Freidlin–Wentzell, entropy

The same machinery scales up to path space and to thermodynamics.

Schilder's theorem

Scaled Brownian motion $\sqrt\varepsilon\,W$ on $C([0,T];\mathbb{R}^d)$ satisfies an LDP at speed $1/\varepsilon$ with rate $$I(\phi)=\frac{1}{2}\int_0^T|\dot\phi(t)|^2\,dt\qquad(\phi\text{ absolutely continuous, }\phi(0)=0)$$ and $I(\phi)=+\infty$ otherwise. The minimum-action path between two points is a straight line — Brownian sample paths "want" to stay still, and any deviation costs Dirichlet energy.

Freidlin–Wentzell

For the small-noise SDE $dX^\varepsilon_t=b(X^\varepsilon_t)\,dt+\sqrt\varepsilon\,dW_t$, replace $\dot\phi$ in Schilder's rate by the deviation from the deterministic flow: $$S(\phi)=\frac{1}{2}\int_0^T|\dot\phi(t)-b(\phi(t))|^2\,dt.$$ The quasi-potential $V(x_0,y)=\inf\{S(\phi):\phi(0)=x_0,\phi(T)=y,T\ge 0\}$ governs rare exit times: if $D$ is a basin of an attractor $x_0$, the mean exit time satisfies $$\mathbb{E}_{x_0}[\tau_D]\asymp\exp\bigl(V(x_0,\partial D)/\varepsilon\bigr).$$ Eyring–Kramers refines the prefactor; transition pathways concentrate on the instanton, the action minimiser through the saddle.

Boltzmann entropy

For an ideal gas with $n$ particles in $k$ momentum cells, the empirical occupation $L_n$ satisfies Sanov: $\mathbb{P}(L_n\approx\nu)\asymp e^{-nH(\nu\,\|\,\mu)}$. Equivalently, the number of microstates compatible with macrostate $\nu$ is $W\asymp e^{nS(\nu)}$ where $S=-H(\nu\,\|\,\mu)$ — recovering Boltzmann's $S=k\log W$ with $k=k_B$ the conversion to physical units. The Gibbs measure $e^{-\beta E}/Z$ is the maximiser of $S-\beta\langle E\rangle$, i.e.\ the minimiser of free energy. Statistical mechanics, entirely from large deviations.

Schilder paths and Freidlin–Wentzell action
drag the endpoint — minimum-action path adjusts
</section>

6. Connections

Large-deviation theory is the precise statement of "rare events have an asymptotic exponential rate". It surfaces in three places that initially look unrelated.

Probability theory and CLT

Probability theory supplies the LLN / CLT-quality control on means; large deviations is the next-order correction telling you the exponential decay rate of the LLN exception.

Information theory

Sanov's theorem identifies the rate function with KL-divergence, which is the same KL-divergence that appears in source coding and channel capacity. The two theories are the same theorem on different sides.

Statistical mechanics

Boltzmann entropy is a rate function; Gibbs measures are the maximisers of an entropy / energy variational problem (Gibbs variational principle). LDP theorems are the modern way to derive thermodynamic limits.

Stochastic calculus and Schilder

SDE solutions with small noise satisfy a Freidlin–Wentzell LDP; the rate function is an action integral. This is one of the bridges to PDE via Hamilton–Jacobi-style minimisation.

Open frontiers: LDP for interacting particle systems (KPZ, hydrodynamic limits); Schrödinger problem and entropic optimal transport (Léonard, Mikami); large deviations for non-equilibrium statistical mechanics (Macroscopic Fluctuation Theory).
